about this work. His quotations from Kdfyapa show that his Sarhhita too
had contained matters relating to erotics which Vardhamihtra must
have utilized for his work.
His proficiency in lexicon is proved by his quotations from
(already referred to). He explains certain words, quoting
relevant Kofas : Kukundara as the cavity of the loins; Dintfibhayda, as
a particular musical instrument; Jatru is explained as 'fenft: tffitT:'
which is palpably incorrect. The mistake must be attributed to the
scribe- For, Kfirasvdmin says(^nft:?) 5^."s He
explains the word Viyadgd as 'girdle.' Similarly about Vifdna he
quotes some Ko^a to show that it means also 'tusk.'4 He follows the
same procedure to prop up his explanation ofKapoiapdliP
His scholarship of grammar is testified by his quotation of
Panini's authority for explaining words like Hautabhuja* Similarly
under LXVI.2 he says, W^R:" which is permitted
by Panini's rule II.4-2,
